title: Depreciation Methods: What is the formula for calculating depreciation expense using the...
summary: Identify the straight-line depreciation formula: (cost minus residual value) divided by useful life.
prompt: What is the formula for calculating depreciation expense using the straight-line method?

options.text: Depreciation Expense = (Cost - Residual Value) / Useful Life
It only affects the balance sheet
It has no effect on net income

solution: The straight-line method of depreciation allocates an equal portion of an asset's acquisition cost over its useful life. The formula is: Depreciation Expense = (Cost - Residual Value) / Useful Life. This method is straightforward and commonly used because it provides a consistent expense amount each accounting period, aiding in budgeting and financial forecasting. Understanding this method is essential for accurate financial reporting and asset management.
